intro: Trump's first administration launched the first-ever comprehensive federal PFAS strategy in February 2019 — the PFAS Action Plan. Under Administrator Andrew Wheeler, EPA made significant commitments and took early regulatory steps. Biden's EPA completed what Trump 1 started. Trump 2 is now rolling back what Biden completed.
sourceTitle: EPA Delivers Results on PFAS Action Plan

actions.title: PFAS Action Plan released
actions.description: First-ever comprehensive cross-agency federal PFAS strategy. EPA called it 'the most comprehensive cross-agency plan ever to address an emerging chemical of concern.'

actions.title: 172 PFAS added to Toxic Release Inventory
actions.description: Final rule requiring industrial facilities to report releases of 172 PFAS chemicals into air, water, and land — expanding public right-to-know on PFAS pollution.

actions.title: TSCA rule: new PFAS cannot enter market without EPA review
actions.description: Final rule under TSCA Section 5 stopping products containing PFAS from reaching the marketplace without EPA permission — a new chemical screening requirement.

actions.title: Interim guidance on PFAS destruction and disposal
actions.description: Released guidance on how to safely destroy and dispose of PFAS-contaminated materials, including incineration conditions and alternative treatment approaches.

actions.title: Formal determination to regulate PFOA and PFOS in drinking water
actions.description: After reviewing 11,000+ public comments, EPA issued final regulatory determinations for PFOA and PFOS — formally initiating the rulemaking process for binding maximum contaminant levels. This set the table for Biden's 2024 MCL rule.

actions.title: UCMR5: proposed monitoring for 29 PFAS in drinking water
actions.description: Proposed the fifth Unregulated Contaminant Monitoring Rule, requiring water systems nationwide to test for 29 PFAS compounds. Results (2023–2025) later confirmed PFAS above new MCLs in systems serving roughly 70 million people.

actions.title: ANPRM: PFAS as CERCLA hazardous substances
actions.description: Advanced notice of proposed rulemaking seeking public comment on designating PFOA and PFOS as CERCLA hazardous substances — starting the formal evaluation process Biden completed in 2024.

actions.title: Over $15 million in PFAS research grants awarded
actions.description: Funded research on human/ecological exposure, environmental risks in waste streams, and impacts on farms, ranches, and rural communities.

note: Trump 1 EPA also conducted 16 enforcement actions and provided technical assistance to more than 30 states on PFAS contamination. The critical gap: the Trump 1 PFAS Action Plan started the MCL process but did not complete it — that was left to Biden's EPA, which finalized binding limits in April 2024.
